ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for white chocolate mocha; mapped to parent class 'coffee beverage' (FOODON_03301079) as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for white chocolate mocha; AN000A (Coffee and coffee substitutes) is the closest defensible category anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept identified for white chocolate mocha beverage; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

allergens: soy (white chocolate sauce may contain soy lecithin)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard tall serving of White Chocolate Mocha is approximately 354 g (12 fl oz), yielding roughly 149 kcal per serving based on the per-100 g values from the HPB Singapore database.

nutrient_source.title: White Chocolate Mocha — HPB Singapore Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo entry for White Chocolate Mocha (cr_id G00010290). All per-100 g values are preserved as reported. Saturated fat is not separately reported by HPB for this entry; estimated from white chocolate sauce and whole-milk composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a secondary reference.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for White Chocolate Mocha in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 (low) by analogy with sweetened milk-based coffee beverages and sugar-sweetened drinks with similar sugar content (~10 g/100 g). The dominant glycaemic driver is sucrose from white chocolate sauce; lactose from milk contributes a lower GI component. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI (55) × available carbohydrate per serving (36.7 g from 354 g × 10.36 g/100 g) ÷ 100 = 20.2.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) in a sugar-sweetened beverage independently raises blood pressure risk; sodium is low at 3 mg/100 g but does not mitigate the sugar-driven BP effect.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit White Chocolate Mocha because its sugar-sweetened beverage status (9.94 g sugar/100 g, exceeding the 5 g/100 g beverage gate) independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance, regardless of its low sodium content; requesting a sugar-free white chocolate syrup and smaller serving size is the preferred modification.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 9.94 g sugar/100 g and zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar from white chocolate sauce is rapidly absorbed, producing a sharp postprandial glucose spike.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid White Chocolate Mocha in its standard form because it delivers approximately 35 g of rapidly absorbed sugar per tall serving with no fibre to blunt glycaemic response; if consumed, a sugar-free white chocolate syrup substitution and a reduced serving size are essential to limit postprandial hyperglycaemia.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from white chocolate sauce (cocoa butter) combined with high added sugar content elevates cardiovascular risk; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased CVD mortality.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit White Chocolate Mocha due to its combination of added sugar (9.94 g/100 g) and saturated fat from white chocolate sauce, both of which are associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased CVD risk; substituting with an unsweetened espresso or black coffee eliminates both risk factors.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory profile: zero dietary fibre, high sugar, and saturated fat from cocoa butter in white chocolate sauce; no anti-inflammatory nutrients present.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid White Chocolate Mocha because its high sugar and saturated fat content from white chocolate sauce are pro-inflammatory, and the beverage provides no anti-inflammatory factors such as omega-3, polyphenols, or dietary fibre; an unsweetened espresso with unsweetened plant milk is a preferable alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: White Chocolate Mocha is a sweetened espresso-based coffee beverage made by combining a shot or shots of espresso with white chocolate sauce (a blend of sugar, cocoa butter, and milk solids) and steamed whole milk. The drink is served without whipped cream in its standard Singapore HPB-recorded form. The flavour profile is sweet, creamy, and mildly coffee-forward, with the white chocolate sauce providing a rich, vanilla-adjacent sweetness that distinguishes it from a standard mocha. Per 100 g, the beverage delivers 42 kcal, 10.36 g carbohydrate (of which 9.94 g is sugar), 0.25 g fat, and negligible protein. Dietary fibre is absent. Sodium is very low at 3 mg/100 g. The high sugar content (9.94 g/100 g) classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), which carries independent cardiovascular and metabolic risk. A standard tall serving (354 g / 12 fl oz) provides approximately 149 kcal and 36.7 g of available carbohydrate, yielding an estimated glycaemic load of 20.2 — clinically significant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or insulin resistance. The beverage is not suitable for people with diabetes, hypertension, or rheumatoid arthritis in its standard form. It is vegetarian and halal-friendly. White Chocolate Mocha is widely available at Western-style coffee chains in Singapore and globally, and is considered a specialty espresso drink in the Western café tradition originating in the United States.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a White Chocolate Mocha?
Can diabetics drink White Chocolate Mocha?
Is White Chocolate Mocha high in sugar?
Is White Chocolate Mocha suitable for hypertension?
How much sugar is in a White Chocolate Mocha?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010290, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. The match is exact. Saturated fat is estimated from secondary USDA SR Legacy data for white chocolate and whole milk components, which introduces minor uncertainty but does not affect the high confidence classification for the primary macros.
notes: protein_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB for this beverage formulation — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database (G00010290); the beverage as formulated yields protein below the reporting threshold at the per-100 g level. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB — this is a liquid beverage with no fibre-containing whole-food ingredients. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero per HPB. magnesium_mg is confirmed zero per HPB. saturated_fat_g estimated at 0.15 g/100 g using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for white chocolate sauce and whole milk in proportion to the reported fat content of 0.25 g/100 g; this is an approximation. The following fields are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific White Chocolate Mocha formulation are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry G00010290, and no equivalent approved secondary source (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, USDA Branded Foods, NCC Food and Nutrient Database) provides these values for this exact beverage formulation: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this entry and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific blended beverage; vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg — full micronutrient panel not reported by HPB for this entry and not available in USDA FoodData Central for this specific blended beverage formulation; phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — mineral panel not reported by HPB for this entry; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB for this entry. All -1 values confirmed unavailable in HPB Singapore Foodo (G00010290) and USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened milk-based coffee beverages from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for White Chocolate Mocha exists in the approved GI source database. Glycaemic load per serving = 55 × 36.67 g carbohydrate per 354 g serving ÷ 100 = 20.2. This is a Western café beverage; no mandatory local-language names apply under the multilingual rules (Singaporean dish rules apply to Singaporean dishes, not Western café beverages sold in Singapore).
